76290.subject: Andy Burnham hits the ground strolling
76290.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDR9AAcAAAAABEUgZWlJAcYva71bJ8nPHd_psWPa_LeZd09tiM3sARxhi1ZLP8ExnkUt5Q6MWahbP_pvP793tSeJj9x-LzlanfMEpv0cH0Uv7KigbQy8AvJ7 


 


 
 

Bo Franklin

Senior digital editor



 Britain’s new prime minister talks of a new era of belief and possibility. He is yet to say what he plans to do. Andy Burnham has so far sacked the closest allies of his predecessor and announced a tax tweak, albeit a small one, to make electricity cheaper. His big ideas for change, which he calls a “ten-year plan”, 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5NDR9AAcAAAAABEUgZWlK2rJkpb381WmgwNFV2N34dBqUAYKCfgUEKi2s0wzP1e5nY_cAyqPm2kTDx_Pr_n0lIEZWPvCrfFN81fz84VkOcJI4gu8f9Yk 
may not come soon .
 


 Supermarkets are easy targets for politicians and hard-up voters seeking someone to blame for high food prices. But look at their profits and it is clear that they are not exactly milking customers. The real cr

Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi

Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-july-21-oleh-bezuhlyi-uhz2f?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BNlg2o6YCSluMOCpg2Wc45g%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3_Onk_KC4LTck1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mruuuhlr~a2-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mruuuhlr-a2
  
🧠 AI’s Crypto News Brief: Market sentiment is bullish as Bitcoin rallies on U.S. regulatory progress and Telegram’s massive wallet integration. Institutional engagement hits record highs at Schwab, while Russia formalizes crypto use for international trade to bypass sanctions. 📈Bitcoin rallies as Bessent signals Clarity Act nearing Senate passage Bitcoin is surging as Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent advocates for the passage of the CLARITY Act before the August recess. The legislation is seen as a major catalyst for regulatory certainty in the U.S. market. Read full story 🏛️Russia’s parliament passes crypto market law with $3,800 annual cap for retail investors The Kremlin has officially authorized companies to use digital tokens for bypassing international trade sanctions and global hurdles. While retail investors face new annual caps, the law marks a significant shift in Russia's economic strategy. See details 📱Telegram to launch non-custodial Gram wallet for over 1 billion users this summer Telegram is integrating a non-custodial Gram wallet into its platform, enabling zero-fee crypto transactions for its massive global user base. This move aims to bring seamless decentralized finance directly to over one billion monthly active users. Read full story 💰Charles Schwab posts record quarter as retail trading jumps 57% Charles Schwab reported record Q2 2026 revenue of $7.1 billion, fueled by a massive surge in retail trading. The firm has successfully expanded into spot crypto offerings, capturing significant interest from mainstream investors. Read full story ⚖️SEC And CFTC Open Joint Consultation On Crypto Derivatives Rules In a rare display of inter-agency cooperation, the SEC and CFTC have launched a joint consultation to define rules for crypto derivatives. This effort aims to provide much-needed clarity for institutional players in the derivatives market. See details 🚨Dogechain to sunset on August 8, users must withdraw assets immediately Dogechain has announced it will permanently shut down its operations on August 8, 2026. Users are urged to withdraw all bridged DOGE and other assets immediately to avoid total loss of funds. Read full story ⚙️Nvidia chips reach customers as company cements 80% grip on AI GPU market Nvidia continues to dominate the data-center GPU market, holding an 81% share as its latest AI chips ship

76281.subject:
AI Ambition Is Cheap. Execution Is the Advantage; AI chatbot that
 took over 8,500 CS roles but no one got fired!
76281.body:
AI Frontier

Автор: Steve Nouri


Автор: Steve Nouri

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-ambition-cheap-execution-advantage-chatbot-took-over-steve-nouri-bxxoc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BPYBH58OzSF6Xf4HBV3vDLw%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3R5z1Hlm7ySYk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrus2fj0~nz-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrus2fj0-nz
  
AI Ambition Is Cheap. Execution Is the Advantage. Generative and agentic AI have made experimentation easier than ever, but launching pilots is not the same as creating business value. The real divide is now between companies that test AI and companies that can repeatedly build, deploy, adopt, and scale it. Most wining companies that I talked with concentrated their transformation on three or fewer priority areas. They did not spread AI across the organization. They focused resources where the economic impact was highest. That is the first lesson: stop collecting use cases. Many companies deploy copilots, agents, and automation tools inside existing processes. This may improve individual tasks, but it rarely changes the economics of the business. The larger opportunity is to redesign complete workflows. Instead of asking how AI can accelerate one step in claims processing, supply-chain planning, customer support, or software development, leaders should ask what the entire process would look like if it were rebuilt around AI. McKinsey found that reimagining workflows end to end had one of the strongest correlations with value. The second lesson is that AI transformation is a people transformation. Agents may take over routine execution, coordination, and some decision-making, but this changes human roles rather than removing the need for strong teams. Companies must redesign responsibilities, retrain employees, and combine human judgment with machine execution. The third lesson is speed. DBS once needed 18 months to put its first AI model into production. It can now deploy one every two months. The advantage is not any single model. It is the organizational system that allows the company to keep moving faster. Finally, AI cannot be delegated to the technology department. Successful transformation requires the CEO to make it a top priority, while business leaders, finance, HR, product, and technology teams share responsibility for delivery. AI becomes valuable when the business owns the outcome, not when IT owns another project. The companies winning with AI are not necessarily using the most advanced models. They are choosing fewer priorities, redesigning complete workflows, preparing people for adoption, and building the ability to move from idea to impact repeatedly. AI ambition gets attention. Execution creates advantage. Strugglin

76279.subject: Is PUMP actually cheap?
76279.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

———————————————————————————

Hi all, happy Tuesday! Crypto extended last week’s divergence on Monday, with BTC and most sectors moving higher even as equities softened. Crypto-linked equities led the move, while AI remained under pressure. 

Today, we also dive into PUMP: up 50% on the month, through its first unlock, and once again sparking talk that the trenches are back. We look at whether the fundamentals justify the move and what the market is actually pricing.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

Monday was broadly risk-on for crypto even as traditional markets softened. The 2025 Crypto Equity Cohort (+5.2%), DEXs (+2.7%), and Oracle (+2.4%) led the sector board, while Bittensor Ecosystem (-2.1%), AI (-1.4%), and Modular (-0.9%) lagged. BTC gained 1% as the S&P 500 fell -0.3%. If sustained, this would extend the divergence highlighted yesterday, when BTC was the only major benchmark to finish last week in the green.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/97f9e082-aeab-417a-bbc6-9f66bea8db6d/image.png?t=1784641255)
Caption: 

All five of the 2025 Crypto Equity Cohort constituents closed higher: GEMI (+6.0%), CRCL (+5.6%), FIGR (+4.7%), GLXY (+4.6%) and BLSH (+2.4%). The likely catalyst came from Washington, where [progress](https://x.com/sjdedic/status/2079472779615699032?s=20) on the CLARITY Act’s merged Senate text, including progress with the White House on ethics language, moved the bill closer to a potential floor vote before the August recess. Senate Majority Leader John Thune has targeted the week of July 20 for action. 

The bid extended to crypto treasury companies, including SBET (+8.5%), BTBT (+7.7%), BMNR (+6.6%) and MSTR (+4.6%). Part of the strength may reflect capital rotating out of crowded AI positions and into names that have significantly underperformed over the past year.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/3dd762e9-f18b-419b-ab20-628c358cebd8/image.png?t=1784641255)
Caption: 

Beyond DEXs and Oracle, Perps (+2.3%), L2s (+2.2%) and DeFi (+2.1%) also outperformed, while Revenue Leaders gained 1.9%. We also saw the rotation back toward Solana continue, although momentum eased. Solana Eco gained 1.2%, while Launchpad slowed from 3.9% on Sunday to 0.7% on Monday as the PUMP rally moderated. 

The broadening of market leadership beyond last week’s PUMP-led move is constructive. Conti
